title: WebView addJavascriptInterface exposes Java methods to JavaScript; untrusted content can call them
Hardcoded value of android:debuggable in the manifest
evidence: Avoid hardcoding the debug mode; leaving it out allows debug and release builds to automatically assign one
fix_recipe: Hardcoding android:debuggable can cause security problems (debuggable apps allow full access via adb). Remove the attribute and let the build system set it.

title: AllowBackup/FullBackupContent Problems
evidence: On SDK version 23 and below, your app data will be automatically backed up to Google Drive; consider android:allowBackup="false" to opt out
fix_recipe: The android:allowBackup attribute determines if an application's data can be backed up and restored, as documented here.

title: Exported receiver does not require permission
evidence: Exported receiver does not require permission
fix_recipe: Exported services, receivers, providers, or activities should either be protected with a permission or explicitly marked as not exported.

title: Using setJavaScriptEnabled
evidence: Using setJavaScriptEnabled can introduce XSS vulnerabilities into your application
fix_recipe: Your code should not invoke setJavaScriptEnabled if you are loading content from any source that is not fully trusted.
